name: Ramaco Resources, Inc.
business: Ramaco Resources is a dual platform critical mineral company that operates and develops high-quality, low-cost metallurgical coal in southern West Virginia and southwestern Virginia, and is developing a producer of coal, rare earth elements and critical minerals in Wyoming. Its primary source of revenue is the sale of metallurgical coal to North American blast furnace steel mills and coke plants, as well as international metallurgical coal consumers. It also targets the processing and production of rare earth elements and critical minerals including heavy magnetic rare earth elements and critical minerals like gallium, germanium and scandium.
sellsTo: North American integrated steel mills and coke plants
International customers in Europe, South America, Asia, and Africa
Premium-priced specialty markets such as foundry cokemakers, manufacturers of activated carbon products, and specialty metals producers

exposures.sentence: Our primary source of revenue is the sale of metallurgical coal.
In mid-2025, we initiated development of our rare earth element and critical mineral operations near Sheridan, Wyoming (the Brook Mine).
Contiguous to the Brook Mine, the Company operates a carbon research facility related to the production of advanced carbon products and materials from coal.
Rare earth elements are critical enablers of advanced technologies including defense systems, permanent magnets, transportation, electronics, and robotics.
Rare earth elements are critical enablers of advanced technologies including defense systems, permanent magnets, transportation, electronics, and robotics.

Demand for rare earth elements and critical minerals may be impacted by demand for downstream products such as hybrid and electric vehicles, wind turbines, robotics, medical equipment, military equipment and other high-growth, advanced motion technologies.

catalysts.event: Ribbon cutting and groundbreaking at Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: We had a ribbon cutting and groundbreaking at our carbon ore and rare earth mine, the Brook Mine, on July 11, 2025.

catalysts.event: Fluor Corporation issued Preliminary Economic Assessment for Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, the Fluor Corporation (Fluor) issued a Preliminary Economic Assessment (the PEA) in which it concluded that the Brook Mine was both technically and economically viable based on its findings and the product pricing information provided by the Company.

catalysts.event: Weir issued updated Technical Report Summary for Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: On September 17, 2025, we received a new geological TRS from Weir, updated from the March 2025 study.

In November 2025, the Company acquired what it refers to as the Russell County property which are contiguous with the Knox Creek complex and which is expected to significantly increase the reserves and resources at Knox Creek.
in July 2025, the Company issued $65.0 million aggregate principal amount of our 2030 Senior Notes
in early August 2025, we raised approximately $200.0 million from an offering of our Class A common stock
in November 2025, the Company issued $345.0 million aggregate principal amount of our 2031 Convertible Senior Notes

catalysts.event: Third Amended and Restated Credit and Security Agreement
catalysts.sentence: in December 2025, the Company entered into a Third Amended and Restated Credit and Security Agreement that increased our available revolving credit commitments to $500 million

catalysts.event: Completion of pre-feasibility study for Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: The subsequent more detailed pre-feasibility study, also being prepared by Hatch, is now expected for completion by late 2026.
